sources_exist.note: All ten canonical URL occurrences resolved. Three URLs recur at distinct input paths and were audited separately.

figures_match_sources.note: The PPAI sales-volume page directly supports $26.78B of 2024 distributor sales and a 54% large-distributor share. The remaining score-affecting chains are unsupported or materially overstated: the AI survey is a 2023 PPAI 100 sample rather than a full-industry 25% estimate, the trend page contains no historical adoption curve, the 2018 HALO transaction discloses no multiple, the Align page does not establish three current consolidators, Gallup does not measure age-60-plus ownership in this industry, and the pricing sources omit the claimed gross-margin and billing mix. The ESTIMATE_HEAVY flag is unresolved for buy_mult, exit_mult, historical_analogs, owners_60plus_pct, and t50_years.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The direction of low-to-moderate capture risk is plausible, but ai_replaceable_share is not. The record leaves all contaminated 4-digit wage shares unchanged, including 8.29% PR and 6.55% market research, despite saying they are marginal, while sales remains only 11.2% and demonstrators only 1.62%. Its unlisted-remainder calculation produces 0.532 before an unsupported adjustment to 0.35, and several automation fractions misread the survey: PPAI reports only 22% using AI for logo/artwork and 11% for product recommendations, not evidence for 85% designer or 75% product-research replacement.

rubric_consistent.note: The deterministic dataset copy, role-share sum, and contribution sum are consistent. The assigned ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag on A, B, M, S, and V reflects small stored-versus-recomputed differences rather than a standalone rejection. However, the catch-all rule is not satisfied because the top three revenue sub-segments and their shares are not actually established, the 4-digit role proxy is not re-weighted, active_consolidators includes a public strategic company and relies on transactions outside the 24-month window, and the multiples are neither segment-comparable nor normalized from disclosed transactions.

cross_checks_honest.note: The uplift arithmetic and PRESSURED terminal discussion are directionally candid. But no_proxy_confirmed says the workforce was adjusted to the dominant sub-segment when the listed wage shares were retained verbatim, and it describes the PPAI survey as stronger evidence for core automation than reported. Pricing-model deflation also treats uncited 30%-40% gross margins, representative portability, procurement behavior, and direct-platform pricing as established billing-mix evidence.

source_audits.note: The January 2023 release supports Align's sale of iPROMOTEu to Champlain Capital and describes iPROMOTEu's distributor-support services. It does not mention HALO, Advantage Solutions, iPROMOTEu's alleged 2024 AIA acquisition, or three qualifying platforms doing add-ons in the last 24 months; it is also stale for a current-activity count.

The January 2025 page reports $1M-$3M EBITDA marketing-agency multiples by category, ranging from 4.5x to 5.5x in its table. It does not report the asserted wholesale/distribution BizBuySell figure, promotional-products distributor transactions, key-person discount, or a normalized 4.5x median for 541890; it is also older than 12 months.

The November 2023 article reports AI use by 59% of PPAI 100 distributors, with varying success, and 78% of adopting distributors using it for marketing-content generation. It is stale, does not cover all 6,397 firms, does not measure material use in the defined automatable functions, and provides no basis for the extrapolated full-industry value of 25%.

The page is dated July 3, 2018, not August 2023, and describes TPG Growth's acquisition of HALO without disclosing transaction terms or EBITDA. It does not support an 8.0x exit multiple, the claimed Advantage Solutions comparable, or a recent $10M-plus EBITDA platform benchmark.

The January 2026 article discusses current demand, margins, tariffs, and product trends. It contains no ASI/SAGE online-catalog adoption observations, no 2003-2010 series, and no evidence that e-ordering or distributor ERP reached 50% adoption in 7-10 years.

Gallup reports that 52.3% of U.S. employer businesses are owned by people age 55 or older and documents broad differences in owners' long-term plans. It does not report the quoted 37% Boomer share, measure age-60-plus ownership in promotional-products distribution, or document a 541890-specific succession shortage supporting value 0.45 and the bonus; it is also older than 12 months.

The February 2025 survey article directly supports 2024 U.S. distributor sales of $26.78B and says large distributors, defined as over $2.5M in annual sales, generated 54% of sales. This narrow citation is accurate, though stale and insufficient by itself to establish pi_dist.

The stale 2023 article supports a 59% AI-use rate among PPAI 100 distributors and notes that platform providers may supply AI tools to distributors. It does not demonstrate that vanguard adoption creates platform competition for smaller distributors or establish key-person portability, weakened supplier-access gates, switching costs, or pi_moat=0.30.

The 2023 release verifies the iPROMOTEu sale to Champlain Capital and describes proprietary order-management technology. It does not say PE is actively consolidating to capture scale against AI-platform competition and does not support the claimed moat mechanisms or score.

The page supports $26.78B of 2024 distributor sales and 2.63% growth. It does not report 30%-40% independent-distributor gross margins, 4imprint's operating figures, commission-rep economics, markup-over-supplier-cost billing, per-order revenue share, or annual-contract prevalence; it is also older than 12 months.

reasons: At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.ppai.org/media-hub/ai-s-role-in-promo-grows-despite-hurdles/), replace the stale PPAI 100 vanguard sample with current evidence or a reproducible size-stratified extrapolation for material AI use across all 541890 firms. The page cannot support current_adoption_pct=0.25.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://www.ppai.org/media-hub/ppai-research-new-year-new-ish-trends/) and inputs.t50_years, provide dated observations with a consistent denominator for ASI/SAGE, e-ordering, or another comparable transition. The cited page contains no historical curve, and the derivation rejects its own 3.4-year calculation as too fast before selecting 6.0 years without a reproducible adjustment.

At inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role, actually re-weight the contaminated 4-digit role mix to the dominant sub-segment instead of retaining every supplied wage share unchanged. Reconcile sales, field staff, PR/research, and the unlisted remainder, and align automation fractions with the cited survey's 22% artwork and 11% product-recommendation usage rather than the unrelated 78% content-generation figure.

At reviewer_note and inputs.ai_replaceable_share, identify the top three catch-all revenue sub-segments with auditable, boundary-consistent revenue evidence and show why one is dominant. The unsupported $16B-$17B distributor-only estimate and a list of unquantified other segments do not satisfy the catch-all rule.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://aligncp.com/news/align-capital-partners-completes-sale-of-ipromoteu/), identify each PE-backed 541890 platform and qualifying add-on within the last 24 months. The cited 2023 sale supports none of the claimed current count, and a public strategic company cannot be counted as PE-backed.

At inputs.buy_mult.url (https://firstpagesage.com/business/marketing-agency-valuation-multiples-and-valuations/) and inputs.exit_mult.url (https://www.ppai.org/media-hub/tpg-growth-completes-acquisition-of-halo-branded-solutions/), provide normalized promotional-products distributor transactions or credible size-comparable comps. Generic agency ranges and a terms-undisclosed 2018 transaction cannot support buy_mult=4.5 or exit_mult=8.0.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://news.gallup.com/poll/657362/small-business-owners-lack-succession-plan.aspx), replace the cross-industry age-55-plus statistic with current age-60-plus evidence for the relevant segment and document an industry-specific succession shortage before applying value 0.45 and the bonus.

At inputs.pi_dist and inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.ppai.org/media-hub/ppai-sales-volume-estimate-modest-growth-in-2024-but-reasons-for-optimism-remain/), source the markup and gross-margin distribution, revenue by billing model, rep commissions, RFP exposure, platform pricing, and pass-through mechanism. Market size and growth do not establish pi_dist=0.35.

At inputs.pi_moat.sources, provide direct evidence for client-book portability, supplier-access gates, switching behavior, direct-platform bypass, and fulfillment differentiation. A stale AI-use survey and a 2023 ownership transaction do not establish pi_moat=0.30.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 5 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (buy_mult, exit_mult, historical_analogs, owners_60plus_pct, t50_years)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S, V
